notes: SHIP v1.14.256 — PORT THE RACK (per HANDOFF-INSPECT3D-RACK-PORT.md, committed to /mocks/ this session with MOCKUP-INSPECT3D-FINAL.html). .255 shipped the BAY faithfully but rebuilt the RACK from scratch: one BoxGeometry chassis + one sphere LED per unit, and the rear was two 'hint cylinders'. On John's device it read as coloured shelves on posts — the exact failure the port existed to prevent. THE RULE for this ship (handoff §1): PORT THE MOCK VERBATIM — every mesh, dimension, material copied from MOCKUP-INSPECT3D-FINAL.html — changing ONLY the data source (live Master slots, not the mock's hardcoded GB300 ZONES) and adding envMapIntensity 0.25 to each MeshStandardMaterial (§1d, the one permitted material edit). ONE spliced block replaced the .255 improvised rack (between the frame build and scene.add(rackGrp)); the frame/posts/rails/back panel + the ENTIRE bay (rig, glass platform, scan wave, ghost fleet, motes, hlBand+edge, bayTick/bayDispose, GL cross-dispose, camera/orbit/raycast/scrub, openRmDevice tap contract) are UNTOUCHED (§4). WHAT LANDED: (1a) per-unit INTERIOR by kind — compute trays get 2 NIC blocks, 2 OSFP cages, 8 drive bays (as ONE InstancedMesh, §5) each with a green drive LED, a BF3 DPU, a status LED, and rack ears; switch trays get 2 switch blocks + a full 4x10x2=80-port grid (ONE InstancedMesh, §5) with ~50%-populated violet port LEDs + a status sphere; power trays get 6 PSUs + 6 fans + 6 green PSU LEDs; generic trays (patch/media/storage/unknown) get chassis + one status LED and NO invented hardware (§3 zero-fabrication — interior detail is decoration keyed to TYPE, never a claim about a specific device). (1b) REAR ASSEMBLY rebuilt for real (was two hint cylinders): cartridge, 2 copper bus bars, top+bottom manifolds + 3 vertical pipes, 6 cable arms, rear door — mock geometry + materials verbatim + env .25. (1c) CABLES ported verbatim (createCable tube/curve/registry + every mock createCable call) HIDDEN by default; a new CABLES toggle is injected into the 3D control strip (.reh-3d-seg styling, id reh3dBtnCables) that flips c.visible across cableMeshes + its own .is-on — it owns cableMeshes directly (no window global), is created only while 3D is live, and is removed in teardown(). Side panels + feet (omitted in .255) added per mock. §1 SIDE PANELS/FEET/REAR use the mock's own materials; frame/posts/rails/back stay CHROME. (2) CHASSIS stays TYPE-COLOUR (§3 + the .251-.253 honesty arc: magenta UNKNOWN, slate MEDIA CONV, gold PDU); the .255 flagged tray deviation is now RESOLVED in the comment (~L31779) with John's device ruling 'keep trays type-colored, magenta unknown looks right' — piano-black trays ruled AGAINST, locked. (3) hgtUnknown still draws the gold hazard hatch (§3, unchanged from .255). ⚠️⚠️ TWO HANDOFF-INTERNAL CONTRADICTIONS, resolved toward the MOCK per THE RULE, FLAGGED LOUD in-code (~L31858 header) for John's device call — BOTH are one-edit flips: (A) REAR FINISH — §1b/§1d/§7 say port the rear materials verbatim (copper bus bars, green cartridge, blue manifolds); §2 says chrome-black the rear assembly/cartridge/door. The mock + §7's 'the rack looks the SAME as the mock side by side, any visible gap is a fail' bar OUTWEIGH §2's one clause, so the rear is ported VERBATIM (COPPER bus bars visible). If John wants chrome-black rear, swap the 4 rear mats (cartridgeMat/busBarMat/manifoldMat/rearDoorMat) to the CHROME const — one edit. (B) EARS — the mock builds rack ears on COMPUTE units (mock L555-562, inside the compute branch); §1a/§7 TEXT lists ears under switch. Ported per the MOCK CODE (ears on compute), because §1's whole thesis is 'port the mock verbatim, the mock is reference truth.' Flip the ear block into the switch branch if John rules switch. §5 MOBILE BUDGET honored: (1) shared materials — every repeated structure/detail material created ONCE and reused across all units (the mock re-allocated per unit); (2) InstancedMesh for the two dominant box-repeats (compute drives 8->1 draw/unit, switch ports 80->1 draw/switch) — identical look, big draw-call cut; (3) LEDs share 2 group-blinked MeshBasic materials (green: drive+compute-status; violet: port+switch-status) driven in bayTick with a scratch setRGB — NO per-frame Color allocation (the mock did `new THREE.Color()` per LED per frame). Estimated ~500-700 meshes on a populated 42U rack (representative: ~16 compute + ~3 switch + ~3 power + generics), ~130 of them tiny LED spheres sharing 2 materials; ACTUAL mesh count + on-device FPS = John's device read (§7 [J]). GL DISCIPLINE unchanged & verified: teardown's scene.traverse disposes every ported geometry+material (InstancedMesh included), bayDispose frees the env cubemap + CanvasTextures, and the symmetric _forge3dActive / _reh3dActive cross-dispose (:31752 / :17676) is untouched — one WebGL context ever. Gates: node --check 4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS; CSS brace balance 3857/3857 (was 3856; +1 = the single .reh-3d-cables rule §6 anticipated); CRLF preserved (bare-LF 0); latin1 byte-splice round-trip clean; InstancedMesh count 2 (drives+ports); exactly 1 scene.add(rackGrp); old 'rear cooling/manifold hint' gone; three-stamp lockstep .255->.256. ?legacy=1 BYTE-IDENTICAL: rackElevation_render3D is reachable only via the reh3d 3D toggle, emitted only under body.rd (.254); the legacy house never enters this function; the one CSS rule is body.rd-scoped. NOTED, STILL OWED (unchanged, owed its own ship): the FLAT rack-elevation CSS is keyed on the EDP vocabulary while master_rackToElevation emits master_hostType RAW codes — intersection only 'gpu' — so FLAT colours only GPUs on Master racks; the 3D rack (this ship + .255) reads _TMAP->TYPE_COLOR and is MORE correct, so 3D and FLAT DISAGREE on non-GPU colour and that is the elevation's bug, not the 3D view's. Also STILL NOTED, NOT FIXED (18+ stamps dormant, re-animated by the .254 rewire, flagged for a hardening ship, deliberately NOT drive-by patched): reh3d_setMode opens `if (!host) return;` — a hard-rule-#1 silent return. BATCH: opens at 6 (.251/.252/.253/.254/.255 + .256) — AT THE 6-CAP. This is the consolidated-device-pass ship; no further stack without a verify pass. .251-.254 were cleared by John ('all good'->'build it'); .255 device-verified [G] trays/magenta + [I] FORGE->3D GL; .256 is NEW/unverified. DEVICE-VERIFY (John, iPhone — the bar is the mock, side by side; handoff §7): [FLAG-FIRST] Rear bus bars render COPPER (mock-verbatim, §1) not chrome — is that right, or do you want chrome-black rear (§2)? And ears are on COMPUTE trays (mock-verbatim) not switch — right, or move to switch? [1] Open MOCKUP-INSPECT3D-FINAL.html and the app 3D view side by side — the rack looks the SAME (any visible chassis-detail gap = fail). [2] Compute trays: NIC blocks, OSFP cages, drive bays with LEDs, BF3, status LED. [3] Switch trays: switch blocks, full port grid, status. [4] Power trays: PSUs + fans + LEDs. [5] Rear: cartridge, copper bus bars, top+bottom manifolds + vertical pipes, cable arms, rear door — no hint cylinders. [6] CABLES toggle: appears in the 3D strip, off by default, tap -> cable tubes appear, tap -> gone. [7] Type colours intact (magenta UNKNOWN, slate MEDIA CONV, gold PDU) — bay never contradicts FLAT. [8] Gold hatch still marks hgtUnknown slots (SPARKS gpu-b40-02 / cpu-gp2-* / fs-media-converter-chassis). [9] Bay unchanged: scan wave, ghost fleet, platform, rig — RACK STILL READS AS THE STAR. [10] Tap a tray -> flat device detail; orbit-drag does NOT trigger it. [11] FLAT toggle -> GL disposed; open FORGE after -> no dual-GL crash; FORGE-first then 3D -> no crash; cold PWA reopen offline -> rack still renders. [12] Frame rate acceptable — note if it drags (pre-approved trims: cables already hidden; drive LEDs static; motes 220->120; reflection->floor fade; NEVER trim the port grid or NIC blocks). [13] ?legacy=1 -> toggle absent, unchanged. STILL OWED — JOHN'S RULING (data, unchanged): DFW02 empty-model row @c1:001:38; heights for gpu-b40-02(160)/cpu-gp2-*(165)/inf-med-01(60)/om2216-c14(16)/fs-media-converter-chassis(5)/VAST DBox(50)/net-ufm-05; whether .252 magenta reads too loud; AND the FLAT-elevation EDP-vocabulary colour bug (owed its own ship).
